key_insight: A hybrid UNet-Transformer can rapidly translate composite microstructure images into accurate stress and strain field predictions, acting as a practical surrogate for FEM.
engineering_relevance: This enables much faster field evaluation for physics-informed composite design and microstructure optimization without running expensive finite element simulations each time.
method: Input: composite geometry images from square, hexagonal, and triangular two-phase tessellations
Model: UNet encoder-decoder with Transformer attention to capture local structure and long-range interactions
Output: predicts multiple stress/strain field types directly from geometry
evidence_signal: The model achieved very strong performance on most subdatasets, with peak results up to R2=0.9991, SSIM=0.9936, and MAE=0.0050, while six of eight subdatasets stayed below MAE 0.05 on the normalized scale.
watch_item: Performance drops on irregular square-grid cases with sparse soft-phase inclusions, indicating difficulty reproducing sharp stress discontinuities in complex geometries.
paper_title: Hybrid Unet-Transformer Model for Generating Stress and Strain Fields from Composite Geometrics
